description: Student submitted a DMIST claiming albuterol was administered, but only O2 was applied — albuterol is absent from the intervention records. The corroboration prepass is provided (prepass_result) identifying the fabricated claim. The evidence packet should surface the unsupported claim and reduce the DMIST ceiling. This tests the existing prepass wiring — the unsupported claim should flow through to corroboration.dmist_unsupported_claims and trigger a prompt-guided DMIST ceiling reduction.

o2_nrb.label: High-flow O2 via NRB mask (15 LPM)

submitted_docs.dmist: D: 8-year-old male, difficulty breathing. M: History of asthma, albuterol PRN. No allergies. I: Applied O2 via NRB 15 LPM. Administered albuterol 2.5 mg via SVN. Patient positioned upright. S: SpO2 83% on arrival, HR 134, RR 32. T: Transporting to Pediatric ED via ALS intercept.
submitted_docs.narrative: C: Asthma exacerbation in 8yo male. H: 2-hour onset of wheezing, history of asthma. A: SpO2 83%, wheeze. R: O2 and albuterol given. T: ALS transport.

student_messages: General impression: respiratory distress. When did this start? Any allergies?

dmist_unsupported.reason: No albuterol in intervention timeline — only o2_nrb and positioning recorded

critical_actions.description: Administer high-flow O2 via NRB mask
Administer albuterol 2.5 mg via SVN per protocol
